title: nephelea pubescens
intro: Nephelea pubescens, also known as the hairy nephelea or hairy mountain artemisia, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestive problems. Nephelea pubescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
